title: v0.9.0: Multi-directory JSONL support — track Claude Code across multiple project dirs
description: ccdash currently reads a single JSONL log directory. Claude Code can be used across many project directories. Add config option or auto-discovery to scan multiple JSONL directories (e.g., ~/.claude/projects/*/*/*.jsonl). Aggregate costs/tokens across all projects in the dashboard.

title: Migrate CI from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows (iad-ci)
close_reason:
CI migration from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows completed.

## Changes
- Removed .github/workflows/release.yml (GitHub Actions)
- Added ccdash-ci WorkflowTemplate to declarative-config
- Documented migration status and manual workflow submission

## Retrospective
- **What worked:** Reused existing Argo WorkflowTemplate patterns from other projects (forge-ci, needle-ci) for consistent multi-platform Go builds with GitHub releases
- **What didn't:** The ArgoCD application has an invalid cluster reference (separate infrastructure issue) — manual workflow submission is a workaround
- **Surprise:** The workflow was already created in declarative-config in a prior commit, so this task was primarily about verification and documentation
- **Reusable pattern:** For Go project CI migrations, copy an existing -ci workflowtemplate (e.g., forge-ci, needle-ci) and adjust: repo URL, binary names, and test commands

title: v0.9.0: Network I/O panel — track bytes sent/received and connection counts
description: Add a Network I/O panel to System Resources showing bytes_sent/bytes_recv rates and active TCP connection count via gopsutil/v3/net. Display alongside Disk I/O for complete system resource picture. Useful for monitoring LLM API traffic.

title: Genesis: ccdash Implementation
description: Lightweight TUI dashboard for Claude Code token usage, agent sessions, and system resources. Go project at v0.6.28+. Has releases. Tied to repo: jedarden/ccdash.

title: Migrate CI: replace GitHub Actions release workflow with Argo WorkflowTemplate
description: ccdash uses GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/release.yml) for binary releases. Violates project-wide CI policy: Argo Workflows on iad-ci only, no GitHub Actions. Create argo-workflows WorkflowTemplate in jedarden/declarative-config k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/ that builds multi-arch Go binaries (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-amd64) and publishes GitHub release on tag push. Disable/delete the GitHub Actions workflow.
close_reason:
CI migration from GitHub Actions to Argo Workflows is complete. The WorkflowTemplate ccdash-ci-workflowtemplate.yml was created in jedarden/declarative-config (k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/). Builds multi-arch Go binaries (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64), runs tests, and publishes GitHub releases. GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml) was deleted.

## Retrospective
- What worked: Following existing WorkflowTemplate patterns made structure straightforward; gh release create with prebuilt binaries is clean; idempotency check prevents duplicate releases
- What didnt: VERSION extraction — initial grep '^version' didnt match bare version format; gh release create paths broke after cd bin (needed ./ prefix); duplicate ccdash-ci.yaml needed removal
- Surprise: A duplicate template file existed from an earlier partial attempt, missing gh CLI installation
- Reusable pattern: For Go binary release workflows — use cat VERSION not grep; use relative paths after cd in shell steps; verify no duplicate template files before ArgoCD sync
